prerequisites: An active Serpstat account at serpstat.com.
An API access token (key) issued for your account, found under your profile via Account management (listed as "Key to access the API").
Available API credits/limits on your account so requests are accepted by the Serpstat Public API.
The domain or domains you want to analyze (set via the optional 'domain' / 'domains' config fields), and optionally a region/search-engine database selection via 'region_id'.

authMethods.name: API token
authMethods.summary: Serpstat's Public API authenticates with a single API token. The connector's 'api_key' secret field holds this token, which Serpstat appends to requests as the 'token' query parameter against the base URL https://api.serpstat.com/v4. Obtain the token from your Serpstat account profile under Account management ("Key to access the API").

setupSteps.title: Sign in and open your Serpstat profile
setupSteps.body: Log in at serpstat.com, click your profile in the upper-right corner, and choose Account management. Your API token is listed in the profile as "Key to access the API."

setupSteps.title: Copy your API token
setupSteps.body: Copy the API access token from the profile. This is the credential Serpstat passes as the 'token' query parameter when calling the Public API (base URL https://api.serpstat.com/v4). Keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied token into the connector's required 'api_key' field. This is the only credential the connector needs to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Configure the optional query fields
setupSteps.body: Set the optional config fields to scope what the connector pulls: 'domain' or 'domains' for the target site(s) to analyze, 'region_id' to select the search-engine/region database, and 'filter_by'/'filter_value', 'sort_by'/'sort_value', 'page_size', and 'pages_to_fetch' to filter, sort, and paginate results. All of these are optional.
